headline: Persistent agent memory and local-first tooling led the week as on-device models and long-running agent patterns advanced together.
body: Agent memory and local-first tools dominated the rotation, anchored by Hermes, an open-source agent running locally on Mac and Windows with persistent memory, skill generation, and cron scheduling, plus a desktop companion that preserves Claude's context across sessions. Google pushed on-device inference forward with Gemma 4, whose 2B model now matches last year's 27B dense model and runs offline through the AI Edge Gallery with multimodal tasks and upcoming MCP integration. Architecturally, three patterns for long-running agents surfaced, including event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation, the last of which broke out alongside encoder-decoder. Foundational technique breakouts in embeddings, visual tokenization, Pearson loss, and backpropagation traced to a walkthrough of Karpathy's bigram language model.

cited_items.title: Gemma 4 and the AI Edge Gallery: On-Device AI Gets an Upgrade
Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
